calendar.matters:
Motion To Vacate Judgment And Set Aside Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
CGC-19-579005 Anil Ravikanti vs. Twitter, Inc.
CGC-21-596299 Kumar Gupta Ravikanti Srinivas Anil vs. Twitter, Inc.
Mr. Ravikanti stated that he had a name change and therefore he is the same person in both cases.

Appearances:
Kumar Gupta Ravikanti (470.717.0811; ravikumarmr101@gmail.com) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Liz Sanchez Santiago, Esq. (415.693.2000; lsanchezsantiago@cooley.com) and Kyle Wong, Esq. (kwong@cooley.com) of Cooley LLP for Defendant via Zoom videoconference.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANIL RAVIKANTI'S Motion To Vacate Judgment And Set Aside Judgment is off calendar. Plaintiff's motion is procedurally improper according to Rule 21.5 of the Local Rules of Court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2020 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Max Alderman, Esq. of COOLEY LLP, T:415.693.2000, for defendant/moving party;
Anil Ravikanti, T:470.717.0811, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf, by Court Call.

RULING: Albeit late, plaintiff's request to argue the tentative ruling was permitted by the Court. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT TWITTER, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as follows:

Defendant Twitter, Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend.

[See word minutes for the Court's full ruling.]

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, email:tonyvaughn@yahoo.com

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Continued To Mar-03-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anil Ravikanti, 470-717-0811 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Maxwell Alderman, Esq. 415-693-2250 of Cooley for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views re: continunace, no opposition and no contest email. Plaintiff argues and says he will file a brief. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:
